summary: M5-SIM-PLAYABLE-LOOP-001 is CLOSED only after PR #112 passed CI, merged into main, local main synchronized to origin/main, and all task child threads were closed. The integrated branch adds deterministic M5 playable loop evidence, explicit M4 runtime save/load support, Node worker_threads JSON boundary coverage, tests, and audit handoffs without expanding into UI/client/desktop, formal M5 passage, manual node battle, telemetry, server/multiplayer, full diplomacy, complex naval warfare, or release/commercial scope.

tests_run.result: Quality, Web, and Browser Smoke passed in 1m49s; Windows Desktop Smoke Artifact passed in 1m0s after CI formatting repair.
Merged PR #112 and fast-forwarded local main to fee2ccfeefd7ad73bbfc37442c4224000ad75496.

tests_run.command: git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/main
tests_run.result: Local main and origin/main both resolve to fee2ccfeefd7ad73bbfc37442c4224000ad75496 before closure branch edits.
All three task child threads returned completed previous_status and were closed.

decisions: Mark M5-SIM-PLAYABLE-LOOP-001 CLOSED because accepted implementation and review evidence are integrated into origin/main via PR #112.
Keep M5 formal passage and manual node battle as future Human Gates; this closure does not pass M5.
Continue autonomous M5 execution after closure merge by selecting the highest-priority dependency-ready M5 task from taskctl ready.
risks: P3: M5 browser/client Worker integration is still downstream scope; this task proved Node worker_threads JSON boundary in apps/sim-runner.
P3: M4 save DTO surface is now explicit and larger; future save/runtime changes must maintain DTO coverage and checksum behavior.
requested_action: Commit closure, open closure PR, wait for CI, merge into main, synchronize main, then start the next READY M5 task.
